content.full_text:
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

December 31, 2004

The Honorable Herbert Higgins
City of Norco
2340 Reservoir Drive
Norco, CA 92860

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-04-268

Dear Mayor Higgins:

This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding redevelopment law and the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹

Please note that under the provisions of regulation 18329(b)(8)(D) (copy enclosed), the Commission does not advise on bodies of law outside of the Act. For example, your question primarily concerns Health and Safety Code section 33130(a) which concerns the acquisition and ownership of real property in redevelopment project areas. This is not a provision on which we can advise.

In addition, it appears that your question pertains to the duties of another public official and the Commission cannot advise third parties regarding the duties of a public official. Thus, under the provisions of California Code of Regulations section 18329(b)(8)(B), unless you are an authorized representative of the person for whom you

_________________
¹ Government Code sections 81000 – 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections 18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.

File No. W-04-268
Page No. 2

seek assistance, the Fair Political Practices Commission cannot address your questions or
provide analysis. For general guidance concerning the Act, however, I have enclosed a
conflict-of-interest fact sheet.

If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: John W. Wallace
Assistant General Counsel
Legal Division

sections.question_synthetic: The requester asked for advice regarding potential conflicts of interest under the Political Reform Act related to redevelopment law and real property acquisition under Health and Safety Code section 33130(a).
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declined to provide advice because the question primarily concerns Health and Safety Code provisions outside the Political Reform Act's scope, and the question pertains to duties of another public official, which the FPPC cannot advise on without authorization from that official.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a declination letter. No verbatim QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections are present in the document. The letter explains why the FPPC cannot provide the requested advice and references applicable regulations.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: The requester asked for advice regarding potential conflicts of interest under the Political Reform Act related to redevelopment law and real property acquisition under Health and Safety Code section 33130(a).

CONCLUSION: The FPPC declined to provide advice because the question primarily concerns Health and Safety Code provisions outside the Political Reform Act's scope, and the question pertains to duties of another public official, which the FPPC cannot advise on without authorization from that official.

embedding.summary: The FPPC declined to advise Mayor Herbert Higgins on conflict-of-interest questions related to redevelopment law and real property acquisition, citing lack of authority over Health and Safety Code provisions and inability to advise on third parties' duties without authorization.
